title: Mobile /reviews comparison table hides Rating, DHM, Price AND the Amazon CTA off-screen with no scroll cue
page: /reviews — "Quick comparison table" (ComparisonTableModern). Also affects any page mounting this component.
issue: ComparisonTableModern renders a 5-column table (Product · Rating · DHM · Price · Action) inside .compare-scroll{overflow-x:auto} with .product-cell{white-space:nowrap}. On ~390px the Product column consumes the full width, pushing Rating/DHM/Price and the orange "Check Price on Amazon" CTA entirely off-screen. There is no scroll affordance (no edge fade, no peek, no swipe hint), so mobile users — the majority of affiliate traffic — see a bare list of names with zero prices, ratings, or buy buttons. The helper line even says "Scroll down" (not right). Confirmed visually in reviews/mobile-01.png.
fix: Add a mobile breakpoint that collapses .compare into a stacked per-product card layout below ~640px (name + rating + DHM + price + a full-width orange CTA), reusing the lg:hidden card markup already present in Compare.modern.jsx. Minimum stopgap: drop white-space:nowrap on .product-cell at small widths, add a right-edge scroll-shadow + "swipe to see prices →" hint, and fix the helper copy — but the card fallback is the real fix because the CTA must be reachable.
file: src/components/modern/ComparisonTableModern.jsx (no mobile branch) + src/styles/theme-modern.css (.compare/.compare-scroll/.product-cell ~lines 803-811); mount at src/pages/Reviews.modern.jsx ~line 190

title: "Best value indicator" caption is hardcoded under ALL columns of the Value row (owner issue #1, CONFIRMED)
page: /compare — Detailed comparison "Value (mg DHM per $)" row (desktop table + mobile cards)
issue: The static string "Best value indicator" renders once per product cell with no winner condition, so it prints under all three columns — including Toniiq's 12.0 mg/$ (the worst value) and No Days Wasted's 37.0 mg/$ (middle) — while only Double Wood (50.6 mg/$) is actually highlighted green with a crown. Three cells simultaneously claim to be the best-value indicator; reads as a duplicated placeholder bug and undercuts table credibility. The cell already computes isWinner (used for the green value + Crown) but the caption ignores it.
fix: Gate the caption on the winner flag, mirroring the Crown: render the "Best value" label only when isWinner, and show a neutral unit label (e.g. "mg DHM per $") otherwise. Apply the identical change in BOTH the desktop cell and the mobile card.

title: Head-to-head "Read comparison" cards render the chart icon ABOVE the title when it wraps, INLINE when it doesn't (owner issue #2, CONFIRMED)
page: /reviews "Head-to-head product comparisons" grid AND /compare "Quick Head-to-Head Matchups" grid (same broken pattern in two files)
issue: The h3 uses className="card-title cluster". .cluster is display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap; align-items:center, so the BarChart3 icon and the title text are wrapping flex siblings. When the title fits one line the icon sits inline-left; when the title wraps to 2 lines, flex-wrap drops the text onto its own line and strands the icon ALONE on the first line ABOVE the title. Result: non-deterministic icon placement across cards in the same grid and across pages. Confirmed in reviews/desktop-04.png (every 2-line card has the icon above).
fix: Stop using the wrapping .cluster for an icon+heading. Make the icon flex:0 0 auto with align-items:flex-start and flex-wrap:nowrap, wrapping the title text in its own span (flex:1 1 auto; min-width:0) so the icon stays pinned top-left and only the text wraps. Apply the SAME pattern to both files so the pages match.
file: src/pages/Reviews.modern.jsx (h3 ~line 313) and src/pages/Compare.modern.jsx (h3 ~line 1049); class at src/styles/theme-modern.css line 469

title: Cheers Restore rating renders as a bare "4" (not "4.0") on cards and in the comparison table — looks like placeholder/missing data
page: Home Top Picks card, /reviews product card + quick-comparison Rating column (desktop + mobile)
issue: topProducts.json stores Cheers Restore rating: 4 (a whole number). ProductCardModern renders {rating} (rating = Number(product.rating)) and ComparisonTableModern renders {product.rating}, so Cheers prints "4" while every sibling shows one decimal (4.3, 4.4, 4.5). A bare "4" next to "4 · 7,972 reviews" reads like an unfinished value and breaks the rating-row rhythm; same bug hits any future whole-number rating.
fix: Format to one decimal at render: Number(rating).toFixed(1) in ProductCardModern (~line 91) and Number(product.rating).toFixed(1) in ComparisonTableModern (rating cell). Belt-and-suspenders: store 4.0 for Cheers in topProducts.json.

title: Double Wood card states two contradictory per-serving prices ($0.80 price line vs "$0.66/serving" pro bullet)
page: Home Top Picks + /reviews Double Wood card (desktop + mobile)
issue: The price line renders product.pricePerServing = "$0.80" (correct: $23.95/30 ≈ $0.80) but the third pro bullet is a hardcoded string "Just $0.66/serving - best value tested". The same card simultaneously claims $0.80 and $0.66 per serving — a factual self-contradiction on a high-intent component.
fix: Fix the stale pro string in topProducts.json (id 2, pros[2]) to "$0.80/serving" or drop the price from the pro bullet so the price line is the single source of truth.

title: Blog read-time renders raw prose strings → garbled/duplicated units ("Estimated 30-40 minutes min read", "15 min min read")
page: /never-hungover blog hub grid + featured card (~35 of 197 cards)
issue: The template renders `{post.readTime} min read`, assuming an integer, but 35/197 posts store strings like "Estimated 30-40 minutes", "15 min read", "30 minutes", "36 min". Concatenating " min read" produces visible garbage on the clock chip and also forces the meta row to wrap to two lines, breaking single-line meta rhythm. Confirmed via metadata scan.
fix: Normalize at render: const rt = parseInt(post.readTime,10); show `${rt} min read` only when numeric, else show post.readTime as-is. Better long-term: clean the source data so readTime is always an integer (strip 'min'/'minutes'/'read'/'Estimated', take first number).

title: Comparison-table DHM column mixes numeric doses with marketing prose ("Most DHM per dose", "DHM + Milk Thistle Blend")
issue: The dhm field is meant to be a comparable mg value (tabular-num column), but Cheers Restore stores "Most DHM per dose" and Good Morning stores "DHM + Milk Thistle Blend". This breaks at-a-glance comparison, left-aligns prose where numbers belong, is factually misleading (Cheers is a 300mg-class blend, not the highest dose), and the dhm-sort `parseInt(b.dhm.replace(/[^\d]/g,''))` yields 0 for these, mis-ranking them.
fix: Normalize dhm to a numeric mg value for every product (Cheers → "300mg", Good Morning → its actual mg). If a blend truly lacks one DHM number, render "—" or move the description to a footnote, not the numeric column.
file: src/data/topProducts.json (dhm for Cheers Restore + Good Morning) + sort logic in src/pages/Reviews.modern.jsx (~lines 107-111)

title: Placeholder author "Research Team (2024)" shipped on the flagship human clinical trial
page: /research — Hovenia dulcis Fruit Extract study card (the only human RCT)
issue: The page's single human clinical trial — the most credibility-critical card — prints the literal placeholder byline "Research Team (2024)" while all 24 other cards show real author lists (e.g. "Chen, S., Zhao, X., Ran, L., et al."). Reads as an un-filled stub on the highest-trust study. Confirmed at research-studies.js:195.
fix: Replace with the real author list, or suppress the byline line when authors is a non-name placeholder.
file: src/data/research-studies.js (record at line 195), rendered at src/pages/Research.modern.jsx (~line 571)

title: First-impression card-anatomy break: the top ~21 blog cards are text-only while every card below the fold has a 16:9 image
issue: The grid gates the image on `{post.image && ...}`. The newest posts (hangxiety, magnesium, ozempic, vodka, whiskey, champagne, seltzer, etc.) have image:null, so the first ~21 cards — the most-viewed scroll position — render as bare text cards while everything lower is fully imaged. The opening screen looks the least finished; a first-impression conversion problem.
fix: Either backfill images for the newest posts (data fix) or give imageless cards a deterministic branded placeholder (brand-soft gradient + topic glyph, reusing the .product-media gradient) so every card has consistent height and visual weight.
file: src/newblog/pages/NewBlogListing.modern.jsx (~line 528) or the post JSON image fields

title: "Participants"/"Duration" use .stat (tight-number) styling but ~19/25 studies store descriptive prose
issue: Research.modern.jsx wraps Participants and Duration in the .stat component (designed for tight tokens like "600+"), but most studies store phrases ("Animal models + cell culture", "Meta-analysis of preclinical studies", "In vitro platelet aggregation assays"), so a stat-styled label sits above a wrapping sentence — breaking the one-tight-token stat contract on nearly every card.
fix: Stop using .stat for these. Render as a plain "Label: value" meta pair (like Methodology/Dosage), or apply .stat only when participants is numeric and route descriptive values to a "Study type" meta line.

title: Cross-surface color-discipline violation: shared legacy header shows a saturated GREEN pill CTA above every modern page
page: ALL modern pages (Home, Reviews, Guide, Research, Compare, Never-Hungover) — top-right "Best Supplements" header button
issue: The modern theme's premise is "orange reserved for the single conversion CTA, green demoted to mark/trust." But the shared header (outside .theme-modern) renders a saturated green "Best Supplements" pill that functions as a CTA — the most saturated element above the fold — directly competing with the page's orange affiliate CTAs and stacking two conversion-color languages vertically. The dark legacy footer creates the same top-and-bottom mismatch.
fix: A/B-owner decision (out of any single component's scope). Either render a neutral/ghost header variant gated on the modern flag, or theme the shared Header/Footer for the modern arm. At minimum reconcile the green header CTA so the variant has one dominant action color; otherwise the experiment isn't visually coherent at the top of the fold.
file: src/components/Layout.jsx (shared header CTA), gated on the modern flag — not the page files

title: Reviews winner emphasis is bound to render position (index===0), so a sort/filter can move "Editor's Pick" off the real pick
issue: ProductCardModern receives isWinner={index === 0} over sortedProducts. When the user changes Sort (Price/Rating) or applies a Best-For filter, index 0 becomes whatever now sorts first — so the orange Editor's-Pick emphasis migrates to a different, possibly weaker product, or the genuine pick loses its emphasis. The primary conversion signal is tied to position, not editorial rank.
fix: Bind winner emphasis to a stable product property (e.g. product.editorsPick === true or product.id === editorsPickId), not index===0, so the badge + orange edge follow the real pick regardless of sort/filter.

title: Two unrelated comparison-table designs for the same job: light brand-soft table (/reviews) vs black-header table (/compare)
page: /reviews ComparisonTableModern (brand-soft header, orange per-row CTA) vs /compare inline table (black --color-ink header, green badges/crowns, no per-row CTA)
issue: The flagship side-by-side comparison appears on two pages in two visually unrelated forms. Compare overrides the .compare header to solid black (background:var(--color-ink)) — a dark-on-light treatment found nowhere else in the modern theme — so a user moving Reviews→Compare sees what looks like two different design systems. Winner convention also differs (orange row-edge vs green cell+crown).
fix: Pick ONE comparison-table visual language. Lowest-risk: drop the black-header override on Compare and use the theme's intended brand-soft header + border-first styling (as ComparisonTableModern does). At minimum align header color, winner-highlight convention, and CTA presence across both.

title: Hero H1 "accent" paints a full 4-word phrase green vs the documented "one green accent word" discipline
page: /guide hero H1 (worst case); also a cross-page inconsistency vs Home/Compare/Reviews which accent a single word
issue: The .accent token is documented as a single accent word, but Guide wraps the entire second clause "Science-Based Hangover Prevention" (4 words) in .accent with display:block — on mobile a 3-line solid-green block — fighting the warm-paper restraint and over-weighting color. Every other hero accents one word.
fix: Reduce the Guide hero accent to a single word (e.g. green only "Science-Based" or "Prevention") to match Home/Compare/Reviews/Never-Hungover.

title: Guide hero before/after pair uses mismatched list markers and mismatched card elevation
page: /guide hero two-up cards ("Your current reality" vs "Your new reality")
issue: Two issues on the paired cards: (1) 'Your current reality' is a plain <ul> with disc bullets while 'Your new reality' uses .proscons with green check icons — bullets vs icons, different alignment, looks half-styled. (2) 'current reality' is a bordered white card while 'new reality' is overridden to brand-soft fill with borderColor:transparent — one bordered, one borderless tinted, breaking the border-first elevation language (and on mobile the borderless card bleeds into the page wash).
fix: Give 'current reality' a parallel marker (use .proscons with the existing desaturated .con marker, or strip disc bullets + left padding), and keep both cards bordered (remove borderColor:transparent or give 'current reality' a matching muted fill) so the pair reads as a deliberate two-tone set.

title: Scenario / mechanism card icons sit mid-height against wrapping titles on mobile (same .cluster icon-alignment family as the head-to-head bug)
issue: Scenario titles use h3.card-title.cluster with the icon as the first flex child and align-items:center from .cluster. On mobile the long titles wrap to 2 lines, so the icon floats to the vertical middle of the wrapped block instead of aligning to the first line — the same icon/title misalignment family as the head-to-head cards.
fix: Set align-items:flex-start (or baseline) on the scenario/mechanism title clusters so the icon pins to the first line. One shared .card-title.cluster override (which also helps if the head-to-head fix is generalized).
file: src/pages/Guide.modern.jsx (~lines 373, 405, 430) or a shared rule in src/styles/theme-modern.css

title: Hero "12 min average read" is hardcoded and contradicts the 18-35 min read-times in the cards below
issue: The hero hardcodes "12 min average read" while the cards immediately below show 18/20/24/25/30/35-min reads — the claim reads as placeholder/inaccurate and erodes the trust the strip is meant to build.
fix: Compute the real average from post metadata (mean of numeric read-times, rounded), or soften to a range / remove the number.

title: Author byline is unstandardized across blog cards ("DHM Guide Team" vs "DHM Guide Research Team" vs "Dr. Evelyn Reed")
issue: The card foot renders `By {post.author}` and the author string is inconsistent across the corpus — three+ variants appear in one grid, reading as a data-entry artifact and undercutting the science-backed framing.
fix: Standardize the author field in post metadata to one canonical byline (or intentionally surface real expert names everywhere). Pure data normalization; no template change.

title: Significance callout sets a block of body copy in green-on-green (off-palette for running text)
issue: Significance text uses color:var(--color-brand-strong) #166534 at ~14px on --color-brand-soft #ECF3EE. It passes AA but reads muddy and contradicts the theme intent that green is a trust accent, not running body copy.
fix: Set the significance body to var(--color-ink) (or ink-soft); keep green only for the "Significance" label/icon.

title: Compare hero forces 4 trust stats into a cramped 2x2 grid on desktop instead of a 4-across row
issue: The four stats are hardcoded to gridTemplateColumns:'repeat(2,1fr)' at all breakpoints, so on the wide desktop hero they stack 2x2 in the center with large empty gutters, looking unbalanced and disconnected from the heading.
fix: Make the column count responsive: 2 columns on mobile, 4 on desktop (media query or grid utility).

title: Compare "Overall Score" crown is keyed off effectivenessScore but the cell displays product.score — fields can diverge and crown the wrong column
page: /compare "Overall Score" row (desktop + mobile Top Choice flag)
issue: The cell renders {product.score}/10 (9.5/9.2/9.0) but the winner highlight is computed by winner('effectiveness') over effectivenessScore (9.5/9.0/8.5) — a different field. They agree today by coincidence; if the max score and max effectivenessScore land on different products, the crown sits on a cell that is NOT the highest displayed number.
fix: Key the Overall Score winner off the displayed field — add a 'score' case to getWinner and use winner('score',...) for that row (or display effectivenessScore) so the number and the crown can never disagree.

title: Compare DHM tie (1000mg = 1000mg) arbitrarily crowns one product; tie-break is render-order, not deterministic
issue: No Days Wasted and Double Wood both have 1000mg, but getWinner('dhm') uses strict `prevDhm > currentDhm ? prev : current`, so a tie falls through to whichever product is reached last, crowning exactly one. Users see a definitive winner on tied data (and the un-crowned product even lists higher "98%+ purity"). Same gap affects rating and dhmPerDollar.
fix: Handle ties explicitly: compute the max, then mark every product equal to the max as a winner (show two crowns on a true tie), or mark none and show "Tie". Apply to all tie-prone getWinner fields.

title: Compare table header truncates product names to the first 3 words, silently dropping meaningful tokens
issue: Headers render product.name.split(' ').slice(0,3).join(' '), so "No Days Wasted DHM Detox" shows "No Days Wasted" (drops "DHM Detox") and the cutoff is word-count, not width — a 3-word product shows fully while a 5-word one is silently clipped with no ellipsis, mismatching the full names used in the selector cards and Category Winners.
fix: Show the full product.name with CSS line-clamp/ellipsis or text-wrap:balance instead of a word-count slice.

title: Global mobile sticky "Check Price" bar adds a SECOND orange CTA and overlaps the last card on multiple pages
page: Mobile /research, /never-hungover (and any page with the global sticky bar)
issue: The shared md:hidden sticky bottom bar adds a second orange "Check Price" button competing with the page's single in-body orange CTA, and because no bottom padding is reserved on the list, the final card is permanently occluded by the bar.
fix: Reconcile the double-orange (suppress the in-body orange CTA on mobile, or restyle the sticky bar) and reserve bottom padding/scroll-margin on the page wrapper equal to the sticky bar height so the last card isn't hidden.
file: global sticky CTA component + src/styles/theme-modern.css (mobile section padding); page CTA e.g. src/pages/Research.modern.jsx (~line 404)

title: Reviews Filter/Sort row: native <select> styled as a button keeps an OS chevron, and Best-For pills wrap raggedly on mobile
issue: (1) The Sort control is a native <select className="btn btn-sm"> styled like the secondary buttons but still shows the OS dropdown arrow, reading as a button with a stray chevron. (2) On mobile the Best-For label + 5 pills wrap across 3 rows and the cluster's justify-content:space-between just left-aligns everything, so the group looks ragged and adds friction before the first product card.
fix: Add appearance:none + a custom chevron to the select (or visibly distinguish it as a dropdown); on mobile drop space-between, set a consistent gap, and consider a horizontal-scroll chip strip or a compact dropdown for the Best-For filters.

title: Hero "How it protects you" eyebrow is overridden to grey, breaking the brand-green eyebrow convention
page: Home (modern) hero, above the Liver/Brain pathway cards
issue: Every other eyebrow on the page renders brand-green per the .eyebrow default; this one is overridden inline to var(--color-ink-soft), so it reads washed-out — a lone grey eyebrow among green ones.
fix: Delete the inline color override so the eyebrow inherits the standard brand-green. One-line deletion.

title: Winner card stacks three orange elements (orange border + orange edge + orange badge + orange CTA), diluting the single-focal-point rule
page: Home Top Picks + /reviews winner card (No Days Wasted Editor's Pick)
issue: The winner card applies a full orange (--color-cta) border AND an inset orange left edge AND an orange 'Editor's pick' badge AND the orange CTA — stacking the protected conversion color across non-clickable chrome, so the eye no longer reads the button as THE action.
fix: Keep orange on the CTA + one emphasis element only. Revert the full card border to the standard warm --color-border (keep the inset edge), or make the 'Editor's pick' badge brand-green. Reduces orange to one structural emphasis + the button.

title: Pros/cons truncation silently drops the deciding con and produces uneven card balance/heights
issue: ProductCardModern slices pros to 3 and cons to 2 by array order, so it drops real cons (Double Wood's 'Single ingredient formula', Cheers' 'Mixed experiences with effectiveness' — arguably the most material) and can show a 3:1 check-to-minus ratio that makes a card look artificially rosy, with uneven bottoms before the pinned CTA. Truncation is also order-based, not persuasion-weighted, so a winner's strongest pro can be cut.
fix: Standardize to an even 2 pros + 2 cons across all cards (matches heights and keeps the honesty signal), and curate topProducts.json so the most persuasive pro is first / the most material con survives the slice.

title: Comparison-table "Price" header is actually price-per-serving — $0.40/$0.48 read like bottle prices
issue: The cell renders product.pricePerServing under a header that just says "Price", so a shopper may read "$0.40" as the product cost. Inconsistent with the cards, which correctly label "$0.40 / serving".
fix: Change the table header to "Price / serving" (or "$/serving"). One-word fix, no data change.

title: Comparison-table Rating uses a hollow outline star, not the filled gold star used on the cards
issue: The table renders a bare <Star> with no fill, inheriting the default Lucide outline (empty) star, while product cards show filled gold (#E8A317). The hollow star reads as a 0-star/placeholder glyph and is inconsistent with the cards for the same products.
fix: Give the table star the same gold fill (color:#E8A317; fill:currentColor) or drop the icon and show only the number. Match the card star treatment.

title: Compare winner-marker uses orange (--color-cta) for a non-CTA, and star gold is hardcoded inline instead of a token
page: /compare table winner edge + selector/table star ratings
issue: Two small color-discipline drifts: (1) the shared .row-winner rule paints an inset orange bar (box-shadow inset 3px var(--color-cta)) on a non-clickable winner row, diluting 'orange = buy'. (2) Star gold #E8A317 is re-hardcoded inline in 3+ places with no --color-star token, risking drift.
fix: Recolor the .row-winner marker to var(--color-brand) (green) so orange stays CTA-only; add a --color-star token and reference it everywhere stars appear.
file: src/styles/theme-modern.css (.row-winner line 811; add --color-star) + src/pages/Compare.modern.jsx (inline star colors)

title: Compare/Research hero stat clusters put words ("Real-time", "Expert") in the big numeric .stat-value display style
page: /compare hero (and inconsistent vs Home/Research which are all-numeric)
issue: .stat-value is a large display-numeral style tuned for numbers; putting long words 'Real-time' and 'Expert' in it looks oversized and breaks the stat rhythm (two numbers next to two words), inconsistent with how other pages render stat clusters.
fix: Make all four stats numeric, or demote the qualitative two to a smaller label-style treatment so the big display size is reserved for actual numbers.

title: Inconsistent secondary-action affordances (ghost link vs bordered button) and hero stars (4 solid for a 4.4 label)
issue: Three secondary treatments on one page: hero 'Read the science' is borderless btn-ghost (under-reads as an action next to the heavy orange CTA), while 'See full comparison' and 'Calculate your dosage' are bordered btn-secondary. Separately, the hero hardcodes 4 solid + 1 empty stars next to a '4.4 avg rating' label, so the icons read 4.0 and contradict the number.
fix: Pick one secondary affordance (or ensure the ghost still reads as a button). For stars, accept rounding (document the convention) or render a partial 5th star for .4.

title: Guide final-CTA makes merchant promises ("Free shipping · 30-day returns") on a non-commerce page, plus repetitive proof-point copy
issue: The Guide sells nothing (links only to internal /reviews) yet the reassurance line reads 'Free shipping · 30-day returns · Thousands of 5-star reviews' — promises the guide can't make, a trust mismatch. Separately, '11 clinical studies and 1,000+ years' is restated ~5 times on one scroll, reading as filler.
fix: Replace merchant promises with claims the guide can back ('Science-backed · 11 clinical studies · No affiliate bias'); keep the proof points in the hero + final stat block and vary/trim the mid-page restatements.

title: Assorted Guide/Research polish: trust-row label drift, link-dense hero, James testimonial blue in-byline link, divergent CTA-band/footer treatments, '+N more' unstyled, mobile divider noise
issue: Cluster of minor inconsistencies: Guide hero says '1000+ years of use' but the footer stat block says '1000+ Years of safe use' (same metric, two labels); the Guide hero lead packs 3 blue links creating color noise under the H1; the James testimonial embeds a blue in-byline link breaking the uniform 'AGE NN' meta of the other two cards; Research has two CTA-band treatments (boxed card vs full-bleed) and a weak all-neutral footer CTA; blog '+N more' renders as unstyled plain text after pill chips; and the mobile hero trust-row dividers float between wrapped items.
fix: Batch as a polish pass: canonicalize the trust-row label set; reduce the Guide hero lead to ≤2 links; remove the James in-byline link for symmetry; pick one cta-band treatment; style '+N more' as a muted ghost chip; hide .trust-row__divider below a breakpoint.
